title: Anthony Marion

{{short description|France international rugby league footballer (born 1994)}} {{use dmy dates|date=November 2021}} {{Infobox rugby league biography |name = Anthony Marion |fullname = |image = Anthony Marion.jpg |image_size = |position = {{rlp|HK|LF|SO|SH}} |birth_date = {{Birth date and age|1994|1|12|df=y}} |birth_place = [[Albi]], Tarn,
Occitania, France |height = {{convert|1.75|m|ftin|0|abbr=on|order=flip}} |weight = {{convert|90|kg|stlb|abbr=on|order=flip}} |club1 = [[Toulouse Olympique]] |year1start = 2014 |year1end = present |appearances1 = 156 |tries1 = 40 |goals1 = 44 |fieldgoals1 = 0 |points1 = 248 |club2 = {{nowrap|→[[Toulouse Olympique Broncos]]}} |year2start = 2015(DR) |year2end = |appearances2 = 10 |tries2 = 2 |goals2 = 1 |fieldgoals2 = 3 |points2 = 15 |teamA = [[French national rugby league team|France]] |yearAstart = 2018 |yearAend = present |appearancesA = 6 |triesA = 1 |goalsA = 0 |fieldgoalsA = 0 |pointsA = 4 |new = yes |retired = |source = {{Cite web|url=https://www.itsrugby.co.uk/players/anthony-marion-33828.html|title=MARION Anthony player stats www.itsrugby.co.uk}} |updated = 27 October 2025 }} '''Anthony Marion''' (born 12 January 1994) is a [[French people|French]] professional [[rugby league]] footballer who plays as a {{rlp|lf}} for [[Toulouse Olympique]] in the [[Super League]] and [[France national rugby league team|France]] at international level. He can also play {{rlp|hk}} or in the halves.{{cite web|url=https://translate.google.co.uk/translate?hl=en&sl=fr&u=http://www.to13.com/actualites/le-to-conserve-anthony-marion/&prev=search|title=TO Keep Anthony Marion|website=www.to13.com|date=6 October 2017|accessdate=24 July 2018}} Marion was the back-up goal-kicker to [[Mark Kheirallah]] for Toulouse in the Championship. ==Background== Marion came through the youth system at [[RC Albi]].{{cite web|url= https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/anthony-marion-commits-to-toulouse/|title=Anthony Marion commits to Toulouse|publisher= loverugbyleague|date=22 November 2016|accessdate=24 July 2018}} ==Club career== ===Toulouse Olympique=== Marion joined the Toulouse "Centre du Formation" in the summer of 2014 from [[Racing Club Albi XIII|Albi]]. He made his debut for Toulouse in Elite 2 in 2014/15 and then seized his opportunity later in the season to progress to the first team, due to injuries to more senior players. Marion signed his first contract with TO alongside [[Maxime Puech]] in July 2015.{{Cite web|url=https://www.to13.com/actualites/le-to-xiii-conserve-deux-jeunes-talents/|title=Toulouse retain two young talents|website=www.to13.com|date=7 July 2015}} At the end of the 2014/15 season, Toulouse had decided to move the first team to play in the English competition. With the next English season not due to commence until March 2016, Marion played the first half of the 2015/16 season in [[Elite 1 (rugby league)|Elite 1]] for [[Toulouse Olympique Broncos]], the Toulouse reserve side who had taken their place in the French competition. In January 2016, Marion joined up with the first team for the 2016 campaign. 2016 was Toulouse's first season back in the English competition. They finished the season top of [[RFL League 1|Kingstone Press League 1]] but lost the promotion final to [[Rochdale Hornets]] before managing to secure promotion to the [[RFL Championship|Championship]] for 2017. Marion played in 26 matches in total, scoring five tries in a season that saw him cement his place in the first team and secure a contract for 2017.{{Cite web|url=https://www.to13.com/actualites/anthony-marion-fidele-au-to/|title=Marion faithful to TO|website=www.to13.com|date=22 November 2016}} Marion, alongside [[Clément Boyer]], made the most appearances for TO in 2017, playing in 32 out of 34 matches and scoring 5 tries. On 7 October 2017, it was confirmed that he had been retained for the 2018 season.{{Cite web|url=https://www.to13.com/actualites/le-to-conserve-anthony-marion/|title=TO keep Anthony Marion|website=www.to13.com|date=October 2017}} In 2018, Marion played 28 times, scoring 8 tries, and earned his first call up to the full [[France national rugby league team]]. His durability and utility value (able to play in the halves, at hooker and loose-forward) was rewarded when TO announced on 10 January 2019, that he had been retained for the 2019 season.{{Cite web|url=https://www.to13.com/actualites/anthony-marion-reste-en-bleu-et-blanc/|title=Marion stays in blue and white|website=www.to13.com|date=January 2019}} The 2019 season saw Toulouse finish second in the table and lose out to fifth-placed [[Featherstone Rovers]] in the preliminary final. Marion played in all 27 regular-season games and the three play-off matches, a feat matched only by skipper [[Constantine Mika|Con Mika]]. He scored a personal record 13 tries and also kicked 15 goals as back-up to [[Mark Kheirallah]]. In the final game of the 2019 season against Featherstone, Marion suffered a serious ankle injury. Despite the injury, Toulouse announced on 29 November 2019 that Marion had been retained for the 2020 season.{{Cite web|url=https://www.to13.com/actualites/anthony-marion-poursuit-au-toulouse-olympique/|title=Marion continues with TO|website=www.to13.com}} Due to his injury, he did not play at all in the short [[2020 RFL Championship]] that was subsequently cancelled due to [[COVID-19]].{{Cite web|url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/championship-and-league-1-will-not-resume-in-2020-but-new-autumn-competition-planned/|title=Championship will not resume|website=www.loverugbyleague.com}} On 7 September 2020, Toulouse announced that Marion had signed a new contract that would keep him at the club until 2022.{{Cite web|url=https://www.to13.com/actualites/le-to-prolonge-anthony-marion/|title=TO extend Anthony Marion's contract|website=www.to13.com|date=September 2020}} In [[2021 Toulouse Olympique season|2021]], he played in 13 of Toulouse's 15 games, scoring 6 tries and kicking 27 goals. He wore the squad number 13 and played most of his matches at loose-forward. On 4 November 2021 it was announced that Marion would remain with the club until the end of the 2022 season. On 15 October 2023, Marion played in Toulouse Olympique's [[Million Pound Game]] loss against the [[London Broncos]].{{Cite web|url=https://www.to13.com/a-la-une/anthony-marion-au-to-en-2022/|title=Anthony MARION extends until 2022|website=www.to13.com|date=November 2021}} ==Club statistics== {|class=wikitable style="text-align: center;" ! Updated 20 November 2021 |} ==International career== ===France=== Marion made his debut for [[France national rugby league team|France]] in 2018, coming off the bench in the 44-6 defeat to England at Leigh.{{Cite web|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/45863248|title=Match report England 44 France 6|publisher=BBC Sport}} He retained his place in the France squad for the [[2018 Rugby League European Championship]] that autumn but didn't play in either victory against Wales{{Cite web|url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/stats/match/france-v-wales-27-10-2018/|title=Match report France v Wales|website=www.loverugbyleague.org}} or Ireland{{Cite web|url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/stats/match/ireland-v-france-03-11-2018/|title=Match report Ireland v France|website=www.loverugbyleague.com}} which saw France qualify for the [[2021 Rugby League World Cup]].{{Cite web|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/46085190|title=France qualify for 2021 World Cup|publisher=BBC Sport}} Marion earned his second cap, scoring a try in the 28-10 victory over Scotland in [[Carcassonne]] in France's final match of the Championship.{{Cite web|url=https://www.scotlandrl.com/2018/11/11/european-championship-round-3-france-28-10-scotland/|title=Match Report France 28 Scotland 10|website=www.scotlandrl.com}} Marion missed France's tour to Australia at the end of the 2019 season due to a serious ankle injury sustained in the Championship Preliminary Final 12-36 defeat{{Cite web|url=https://www.rugby-league.com/article/55707/featherstone-produce-masterclass-to-progress-|title=Match report Toulouse 12 Featherstone 36|website=www.rugby-league.com}} to [[Featherstone Rovers]] in the final game of the season.{{Cite web|url=https://www.to13.com/actualites/le-to-prolonge-anthony-marion/|title=TO extend Anthony Marion's contract|website=www.to13.com}} Marion made his third appearance for France in the 10-30 defeat to England in Perpignan in 2021.{{Cite web|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/match/EVP3744700|title=Match report France 10 England 30|publisher=BBC Sport}} ==International statistics== {|class=wikitable style="text-align: center;" !
